get.summary: ListLocals returns a list of Locals.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The maximum number of local repositories to return.
The service may return fewer than this value.
If unspecified, at most 100 local repositories will be returned.
The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListLocals` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListLocals` must match
the call that provided the page token.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
An expression for filtering the results of the request.
The fields eligible for filtering are:

* `display_name`

Some examples of using filters are:

  * `display_name="local"`  --> The local has the display name "local".
  * `display_name="local*"` --> The local has the prefix "local" in its display name.

post.summary: CreateLocal creates a new Local repository.

parameters.name: localId
parameters.description:
The ID to use for the local repository (optional).
If provided, the full resource name will be `locals/{local_id}`.

requestBody.description:
The local repository to create.
The `name` field in the local is ignored.

post.summary: BatchGetLocals retrieves a list of locals based on their name.

get.summary: ListMirrors returns a list of Mirrors.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The maximum number of mirrors to return.
The service may return fewer than this value.
If unspecified, at most 100 mirrors will be returned.
The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListMirrors` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListMirrors` must match
the call that provided the page token.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
An expression for filtering the results of the request.
The fields eligible for filtering are:

* `display_name`

Some examples of using filters are:

  * `display_name="mirror"`  --> The mirror has the display name "mirror".
  * `display_name="mirror*"` --> The mirror has the prefix "mirror" in its display name.

post.summary:
CreateMirror creates a new Mirror.
It validates the upstream but does not initiate synchronization.

parameters.name: mirrorId
parameters.description:
The ID to use for the mirror (optional).
If provided, the full resource name will be `mirrors/{mirror_id}`.

requestBody.description:
The mirror to create.
The `name` field in the mirror is ignored.

post.summary: BatchGetMirrors retrieves a list of mirrors based on their name.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The maximum number of publication targets to return.
The service may return fewer than this value.
If unspecified, at most 100 publication targets will be returned.
The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListPublicationTargets` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListPublicationTargets` must match
the call that provided the page token.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
An expression for filtering the results of the request.
The fields eligible for filtering are:

* `display_name`

Some examples of using filters are:

  * `display_name="target"`  --> The publication target has the display name "target".
  * `display_name="target*"` --> The publication target has the prefix "target" in its display name.

parameters.name: publicationTargetId
parameters.description:
The ID to use for the publication target (optional).
If not provided, the server will generate one.

post.summary: BatchGetPublicationTargets retrieves a list of publication targets based on their name.

get.summary: ListPublications returns a list of Publications.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The maximum number of publications to return.
The service may return fewer than this value.
If unspecified, at most 100 publications will be returned.
The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListPublication` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListPublication` must match
the call that provided the page token.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
An expression for filtering the results of the request.
The fields eligible for filtering are:

* `source`
  * `publication_target`

Some examples of using filters are:

  * `source="locals/123"`  --> The publication has source "locals/123".
  * `publication_target="publicationTargets/456"` --> The publication has target "publicationTargets/456".

parameters.name: publicationId
parameters.description: The ID to use for the publication (optional).

Follows the [Reprepro/Aptly language](https://www.aptly.info/doc/feature/query/).

filterWithDeps.description: Indicates if dependencies of matching packages should be included.

skipArchitectureCheck.description: Indicates if the architecture list verification should be skipped.

skipComponentCheck.description: Indicates if the component list verification should be skipped.

preserveSignatures.description: Indicates if the mirror is a signature-preserving mirror.

PublishPublication triggers the actual publication process.

get.summary: ListMirrorPackages retrieves a list of packages available in the Mirror.

parameters.name: parent_1
parameters.description:
The parent, which owns this collection of packages.
Format: "mirrors/{mirror}"

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The maximum number of packages to return.
The service may return fewer than this value.
If unspecified, at most 100 packages will be returned.
The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListMirrorPackages` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListMirrorPackages` must match
the call that provided the page token.

get.summary: ListLocalPackages retrieves a list of packages available in the Local.

parameters.description:

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
The maximum number of packages to return.
The service may return fewer than this value.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListLocalPackages` call.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListLocalPackages` must match

patch.summary: UpdatePublication updates the details of a Publication.

parameters.name: publication.name
parameters.description:
The resource name of the publication.
Format: "publications/{uuid}"

displayName.description: The human-readable name used to reference this publication.

publicationId.description: The publication ID (UUID) is exposed separately for convenience.

publicationTarget.title:
The resource name of the publication target.
Format: "publicationTargets/{publication_target}"

source.title:
The resource name of the source mirror or local.
Format: "mirrors/{mirror}" or "locals/{local}"

distribution.description:
The name of the distribution.
Inferred from the source if omitted.

label.description: Value for the `Label:` field in the Release file.

origin.description:
Value for the `Origin:` field in the Release file.
Inferred from the source if omitted.

architectures.description:
List of architectures to publish.
Defaults to all source architectures if omitted.

acquireByHash.description: Provides index file by hash if unique.

butAutomaticUpgrades.description: Sets `ButAutomaticUpgrade: yes|no` in the Release file if provided.

notAutomatic.description: Sets `NotAutomatic: yes|no` in the Release file if provided.

multiDist.description: Indicates if multiple distributions are supported.

skipBz2.description: Indicates if bz2 compression should be skipped for index files.

skipContents.description: Indicates if content indexes should not be generated.

publishTime.description: The timestamp of the last successful publication.

lastOperation.title: The last associated LRO with the publication

patch.summary: UpdatePublicationTarget replaces (full replacement) an existing PublicationTarget.

parameters.name: publicationTarget.name
parameters.description:
The resource name of the publication target.
Format: "publicationTargets/{publication_target}"

publicationTargetId.description: The publication_id (uuid) is part of the `name`, and is exposed separately for convenience.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

FilesystemTarget.LinkMethod.description:
Possible link methods for published files. Defaults to HARDLINK.

 - HARDLINK: Create hardlinks for published files.
 - SYMLINK: Create symlinks for published files.
 - COPY: Copy published files.

armor.description: The GPG key in ASCII armor format.

isPrivate.description: Indicates if the key holds a private component.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

Operation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

name.title:

publicationId.description: The publication ID (UUID) is exposed separately for convenience.

The resource name of the publication target.

source.title:
The resource name of the source mirror or local.

distribution.description:
The name of the distribution.
Inferred from the source if omitted.

label.description: Value for the `Label:` field in the Release file.

origin.description:
Value for the `Origin:` field in the Release file.

acquireByHash.description: Provides index file by hash if unique.

Publication represents the configuration for publishing Debian artifacts

PublicationService.PublishPublicationBod.description: PublishPublicationRequest is the request message for PublishPublication.

name.title:
The resource name of the publication target.
Format: "publicationTargets/{publication_target}"

publicationTargetId.description: The publication_id (uuid) is part of the `name`, and is exposed separately for convenience.

PublicationTarget.description: PublicationTarget represents a storage destination (currently supports S3 or Swift).

region.description: The AWS region for the S3 bucket.

bucket.description: The name of the S3 bucket.

endpoint.description:
Hostname used for S3 compatible storage.
If set, the region is ignored.

awsAccessKeyId.description: AWS credentials (Access Key ID) to access the S3 bucket.

awsSecretAccessKey.description: AWS credentials (Secret Access Key) to access the S3 bucket.

prefix.description:
Prefix used in the bucket.
Defaults to the bucket root if not specified.

acl.description: ACL (Access Control List) used for published files.

storageClass.description: The AWS S3 storage class to use.

encryptionMethod.description:
Server-side encryption method. Defaults to none.
Currently, the only available encryption method is `AES256`.

plusWorkaround.description: Workaround for specific S3-compatible issues (e.g. "+" in filenames).

disableMultiDel.description:
Disables the `MultiDel` S3 API.
Useful for S3-compatible cloud storage providers that do not support it.

forceSigV2.description:
Disables Signature V4 support.
Useful for S3-compatible cloud storage providers that do not support SigV4.

debug.description: Enables debug logging for S3 operations.

S3Target.description: S3Target represents an S3 storage destination.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

Status.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

prefix.description:
Prefix used in the container.
Defaults to the container root if not specified.

authUrl.description: The full URL of the Keystone server (including port and version).

tenantDomain.description: The OpenStack domain name the tenant/project belongs to.

tenantDomainId.description: The OpenStack domain ID the tenant/project belongs to.

SwiftTarget.description: SwiftTarget represents a Swift (OpenStack) storage destination.
